northStar: Feature complete ADO provider that has data and resources defined for all resources available in the ADO API
instructions: Build and test only the package you're changing (`go test -tags all ./azuredevops/internal/service/<pkg>/...`); the whole-tree `go build ./...` / `go vet ./...` fill the drive. Make the implementation satisfy the tests as written, and write fixtures with non-default values asserted via a read-back. Commit your work; leave git history intact (no resets). The Release API lives on vsrm.dev.azure.com; in Read, treat a 404 as deleted by clearing the id. Project-action skills live under `forge/skills/`; record each initiative's plan + demo under `forge/history/<initiative-id>/` so the repo carries a browsable build record.
$comment: forge per-project config. The quality gate is single-sourced from .forge/quality_gate_cmd (preflight AND loadProjectConfig read the sidecar); this file no longer mirrors the key. The gate is the FAST, deterministic, creds-free OFFLINE unit suite scoped to the fork's net-new packages (release + taskagent) — NOT the live TF_ACC acceptance tests (those need a PAT + are 120m-timeout, so they run as sharp per-WI gates / operator confirmation, never as the project default). This pairing is the project-scoped fix for the 2026-05-23 dogfood false-pass (brain: themes/2026-05-23-dogfood-cycle-false-pass-gate.md).
$quality_gate_cmd_note: Schema marks quality_gate_cmd required, but THIS file intentionally omits it: the gate is single-sourced from the `.forge/quality_gate_cmd` sidecar (`go test -tags all -count=1 ./azuredevops/internal/service/release/... ./azuredevops/internal/service/taskagent/...`). loadProjectConfig folds the sidecar in before validation, so the merged config has it. mdtoc (the other example) shows the inline form.
$ci_gate_comment: The project's FULL CI verification — mirrors the GitHub workflows (golint + terrafmt + unit-test). A 'make CI green' / quality initiative MUST use this as its per-WI quality_gate_cmd (NOT a narrow lint-only proxy), so forge's own gate goes red until CI is genuinely green. `make test` = gofmt-check + `go test ./...` (whole module, incl. provider_test.go resource-count). Auto-fixers for the formatting checks: `make fmt` (gofmt) + `make terrafmt`.
ci_gate: make test && golangci-lint run ./... && make terrafmt-check
$ci_fix_cmd_comment: The project's auto-formatters, applied best-effort before ci_gate runs so the PR is fmt-clean: `make fmt` (gofmt) + `make terrafmt` (HCL in docs/examples). Mirrors the formatting checks ci_gate enforces.
$ci_gate_unset_env_comment: Env vars STRIPPED before ci_gate/ci_fix_cmd run, so the final CI delivery gate mirrors GitHub CI (a clean env) regardless of the cycle env. The serve env sets TF_ACC=1 so per-WI live-acceptance gates run; without stripping it here, `make test` would run the WHOLE live acc suite (env-dependent failures GitHub never sees, because GitHub runs make test without TF_ACC → acc tests skip → green). See forge contract A3.
$acceptance_gate_comment: Live-acceptance gate config (contract C7). `match` and `requires_env` are always active: when a WI's quality_gate_cmd targets the acceptance suite (path contains `acceptancetests`), the dev-loop errors fast if any of the three required env vars are absent. `required` is ADVISORY (false): the PM adds a live-acceptance WI ONLY when the initiative actually ships or changes live ADO behaviour (a resource or data-source). Analysis/audit/docs/CI/unit-only initiatives (e.g. release-definition-schema-audit) are NOT forced to include a live-acc WI. The architect/PM is guided by the brain (council-constraints.md two-gate model) to add a live-acc WI when ACs involve live ADO; the hard PM gate is not needed when the standing_work_item_acs already encode the expectation as body-injected guidance.
$acceptance_gate_requires_env_comment: TF_ACC must be set for a live-acc gate to actually run — without it, `resource.Test` SKIPS and `go test` prints `ok pkg 0.00s`, which the dev-loop's no-work scan can't catch (it looks like a pass). Listing it makes the dev-loop ERROR a live-acc WI gate whose env lacks TF_ACC, instead of false-passing. Added after the daemon ran INIT-3/4 cycles without TF_ACC and shipped unverified resources (2026-06-06). Extended 2026-06-07 to ALL three vars the PreCheck (commons.go:56-57) demands — TF_ACC was set but AZDO_ORG_SERVICE_URL/AZDO_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN were not listed, so the release-folder-data-source cycle's live-acc gate burned 5 iterations failing in PreCheck on the missing PAT (a t.Fatal, not a skip) and WI-2 ended status:failed yet the cycle still merged unverified-live. Listing all three makes the guard ERROR fast on the real gap.
$acceptance_gate_required_note: Set true 2026-06-18 (capstone): the release-refine initiatives all change resource schemas/HCL surface, where unit+CI gates are blind to live-only failures (e.g. ConfigMode:Attr makes nested attrs required at apply). required:true forces the PM to emit a live-acc WI gated on the TF_ACC test, so the dev iterates against the real live failure.
$standing_work_item_acs_comment: Static testing invariants forge appends to EVERY work item (contract A2). Encodes the two-gate model as standing ACs so it no longer depends on per-WI PM judgment. The dev-loop must satisfy both; the reviewer must confirm both.
standing_work_item_acs: Live acceptance: the change is exercised by a TF_ACC acceptance test against real ADO — `terraform apply` → assert the object via the provider read → mandatory idempotency re-plan (`ExpectNonEmptyPlan: false`) → clean `destroy`. New resource/data-source ⇒ a new `TestAcc*`; change to an existing one ⇒ an updated `TestAcc*`. The WI that proves this change MUST set its `quality_gate_cmd` to the LIVE acceptance command (`go test -tags all -run TestAcc<Name> ./azuredevops/internal/acceptancetests/`, which runs live via the serve env's TF_ACC) — an offline gomock gate cannot catch live-only failures (e.g. ConfigMode:Attr making nested attrs required at apply).

Live evidence: during the acceptance test's live read-back (before destroy), call `_ = testutils.CaptureLiveEvidence(label, url, apiResponse)` (template: `resource_task_group_test.go` `captureTaskGroupEvidence`) so `.forge/live-evidence/<label>.json` is written and `forge demo render` back-fills it into `demo.json`. Use label `acceptance-resource` to match the unifier's checkpoint. For release resources build the vsrm-host GET URL with the release client. The cycle's `demo.json` MUST end with a checkpoint carrying `liveEvidence.url` (a real REST GET), not a test-name table.

CI-equivalent (push-green): the same checks GitHub runs must pass — `make test` (gofmt + whole-module `go test`, NO TF_ACC) + `golangci-lint run ./...` + `make terrafmt-check`. The production resource `.go` carries NO build tag; only `_test.go` files carry `//go:build`; `provider_test.go`'s resource-count list includes any new resource.

Registry docs current: a schema change is NOT done until the published Terraform docs describe it. Run `make docs` (tfplugindocs) so `docs/resources/<resource>.md` + `docs/data-sources/*` document EVERY new/changed attribute, then `git checkout -- docs/guides/` (tfplugindocs deletes the hand-written guides — restore them). Add/refresh the `examples/resources/<resource>/resource.tf` example that the docs embed. Do this BEFORE the PR is opened.

demo.$shape_comment: `live-external`: betterado stands up real resources in a live ADO org (creds in secrets.env) visible via the REST API AND the dev.azure.com portal. The demo is LIVE EVIDENCE — terraform apply -> API GET (liveEvidence.url) + portal screenshots -> destroy — codified in the ado-demo skill, which the forge demo phase composes. The harness unit run is the creds-free FALLBACK when credentials are absent (documented in essence). The demoProcess capture/verify steps drive the executed demo.

name: terraform-provider-betterado

demoProcess.text: Project tests green in CI after merge

$artifactRoot_comment: Committed forge artifacts (Brain 3 + development history) live under forge/ — a single visible home, out of the provider source tree. forge/brain/ is Brain 3; forge/history/<initiative-id>/ holds each cycle's plan + demo. Runtime scratch (_architect/, AGENT.md, worktree demo/) stays gitignored.
$releaseProcess_comment: Translates CLAUDE.md 'Release — publish after a PR merges to main'. In-cycle: regenerate registry docs (make docs) + author the changelog DRAFT. Pre-merge: bump PROVIDER_VERSION.txt (semver). Tag + GoReleaser publish stay in CI (.github/workflows/tag-on-changelog.yml tags on the finalised CHANGELOG push to main; release.yml runs GoReleaser on the tag) — forge NEVER runs tag/publish.

steps.text: Regenerate the Terraform Registry docs from the schema so docs/resources/ + docs/data-sources/ describe every new/changed attribute, then restore the hand-written guides tfplugindocs deletes. Refresh the examples/ the docs embed.

Add a DRAFT changelog entry under ## Unreleased — one bullet per user-visible behaviour change, categorised (FEATURES / ENHANCEMENTS / BUG FIXES / NOTES). The finaliser promotes it to a versioned section post-approval.
Bump PROVIDER_VERSION.txt (semver: minor for new features/resources/attributes, patch for fixes). The tag-on-changelog CI workflow reads this file to cut the v<version> tag on merge to main.
